channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries wins reelection as House Democratic leader
item.description: House Leader Hakeem Jeffries won reelection Tuesday as the Democratic leader, receiving support from his colleagues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Massive groups of illegal immigrants nabbed at border amid fears of pre-Trump border surge
item.description: Authorities have encountered a number of large groups at the southern border in recent days, amid ongoing concerns about a pre-Trump surge at the border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US defense chief says alliance with Philippines will transcend administrations
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin has condemned China's dangerous actions against the Philippines and renewed a warning that the United States would defend its treaty ally if Filipino forces come under an armed attack in the disputed South China Sea.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italian Premier Meloni's coalition suffers setbacks in 2 regional elections
item.description: Italy's Premier Giorgia Meloni suffered election setbacks in two regions as the opposition Democratic party won a tight race in the central Umbria region and deepened its control of the traditional left-leaning stronghold of Emilia-Romagna.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Takeaways from the AP's investigation into Osprey safety issues
item.description: The Osprey is back in the air after being grounded for months following a crash last November that killed eight U.S. service members in Japan.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Kevin Vineys And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Osprey's safety issues spiked over five years and caused deaths. Pilots still want to fly it
item.description: The Osprey is back in the air after being grounded for months following a crash last November that killed eight U.S. service members in Japan.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Kevin Vineys And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden seeks nearly $100 billion in emergency disaster aid after Hurricanes Helene and Milton
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is requesting nearly $100 billion in emergency disaster aid after Hurricanes Helene and Milton, and other natural disasters, telling lawmakers that the money is “urgently needed.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Defense pick Peter Hegseth paid accuser to protect his career, his lawyer confirms
item.description: The Fox News host reached a settlement to resolve an accusation against him, but a recently released memo brought the issue back into the public eye.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin Republican Eric Hovde concedes defeat to Democrat Tammy Baldwin in US Senate race
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin Republican Eric Hovde conceded defeat on Monday to Democratic incumbent Tammy Baldwin in their U.S. Senate race, saying he did not want to "add to political strife through a contentious recount” even though he raised debunked election conspiracies.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: As China cracks down on bookstores at home, Chinese-language booksellers are flourishing overseas
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Yu Miao smiles as he stands among the 10,000 books crowded on rows of bamboo shelves in his newly reopened bookstore.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio colleges could be banned from asking student pronouns on applications
item.description: Ohio's public universities could be banned from asking prospective students their preferred pronouns on applications.
item.dc:creator: David Rees, NewsNation

item.title: Accused of violating worker rights, SpaceX and Amazon go after labor board
item.description: SpaceX and Amazon are asking the Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals to find the National Labor Relations Board unconstitutional. The federal agency is tasked with enforcing workers' right to organize.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Just before the election, a poll from Selzer sent shockwaves nationwide after it found Vice President Harris leading President-elect Trump in Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic AGs rush to form line of defense against Trump
item.description: Democratic attorneys general across the country are readying their legal defenses against the incoming Trump administration, preparing to pounce on potential violations and even take the president-elect to court if he implements controversial policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Florida congressional leaders demand answers from FEMA political scandal
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - Republican and Democratic leaders on Capitol Hill are preparing for a hearing next week with FEMA administrator Deanne Criswell, regarding alleged political discrimination after hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Karoline Leavitt to be youngest White House press secretary in history
item.description: Leavitt will be the youngest person to ever hold the job and is expected to deliver press briefings based on Trump’s statement late Friday that he has “utmost confidence she will excel at the podium.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Josh Gottheimer joins NJ governor race
item.description: SOUTH HACKENSACK, N.J. (PIX11) - The race for New Jersey governor thickened Friday as Democratic U.S. Rep. Josh Gottheimer officially threw his hat in the ring.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Clark County finds 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots in secure vault
item.description: Clark County elections officials discovered more than 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots Thursday, more than a week after Election Day, a spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump spokesperson Steven Cheung to serve as White House communications director
item.description: Steven Cheung, who has served as a top press aide on the Trump campaign for the past two years, will join the incoming administration as White House communications director, President-elect Trump announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Politics Friday: Trump’s path to the White House began at the Iowa Caucuses
item.description: MPR News senior politics reporter Clay Masters looks back on the 2024 election campaign for the Iowa Caucus to election events leading up to this week when President-elect Trump stepped back into the White House.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, Matthew Alvarez, and Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: What Trump's pledge to close Dept. of Education means for students, GOP-led states
item.description: President-elect Trump promised to close the Department of Education. We asked several education policy experts what the impacts of doing so would mean for students and the country.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep and Taylor Haney, MPR News

item.title: What to know North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum, tapped by Trump to run the Interior Department
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum to lead the Interior Department. The two-term Republican governor of the sparsely populated state has taken a business-oriented approach to his tenure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Jacky Rosen looks forward to 2nd term with focus on economy; Nevada senator strongly opposes mass deportation
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Jacky Rosen plans to focus on the economy and kitchen-table issues after voters re-elected her to a second term in Congress, she told 8 News Now.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Thune elected Senate majority leader
item.description: Sen. John Thune (R-S.D.) has been elected Senate majority leader, setting the stage for him to replace retiring Sen. Mitch McConnell (Ky.), who has held the top Senate GOP leadership job for the past 18 years. Thune has served as Senate Republican whip, the No.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9
